title: Reconcile stale spec/lichen-native raw-frame/config draft with LCI CoAP contract
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review. The current app/LCI contract is IPv6 + CoAP over SLIP/BLE/IP per spec/11-lci.md and docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md, while spec/lichen-native is identified as an older CBOR native protocol draft. The draft also defines raw_rx_enable as config key 64 in 10-raw-frame.md but omits it from 04-config.md. Decide whether to retire, clearly mark, or reconcile spec/lichen-native, and if raw diagnostics remain needed, define the CoAP resource/permission model for raw RX/TX arming with tests/docs. No hardware required.
notes:
Evidence from project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review: docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md lines 82-90 say direct native BLE LCI is SLIP-framed IPv6 over a LICHEN-specific GATT service and is not the older CBOR native protocol draft in spec/lichen-native. spec/11-lci.md section 17 says LCI treats the local connection as another IPv6 interface, uses CoAP everywhere, and exposes /.well-known/core, /config, /config/radio, /config/identity, /status, /status/neighbors, /status/routes, /keys, /mesh via CoAP/Observe. spec/lichen-native/10-raw-frame.md says raw_rx_enable is config key 64, but spec/lichen-native/04-config.md does not list that config key. Until this is reconciled, Python TUI project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 treats spec/lichen-native as non-authoritative for implementation and leaves raw diagnostics disabled/advanced placeholder only.
No-hardware implementation evidence (2026-07-02): reconciled LCI/native contract by making spec/11-lci.md the authoritative IPv6+CoAP native app contract and marking spec/lichen-native/*.md historical/non-authoritative for LCI. Added optional CoAP raw diagnostics model: GET /diag summary, GET/PUT /diag/raw/rx status+finite arming, GET Observe /diag/raw/rx/events events, POST /diag/raw/tx transmit, admin-only /diag/raw/* access, LE Secure Connections/OSCORE requirements, rate/regulatory constraints, and no legacy CBOR fallback. Labeled Python and Zephyr legacy native CBOR framing surfaces as historical/prototype compatibility, not current LCI. Updated Python support docs/README and BLE owner wording. Added python/tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py contract guard. Created follow-ups project-LICHEN-py4c.1.17 (typed Python raw diagnostics client/UI flows) and project-LICHEN-py4c.1.18 (legacy CBOR native lifecycle decision).
Validation evidence (2026-07-02, local no-hardware): from python/: uv run pytest tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py tests/client/test_lci.py tests/client/test_ble.py tests/client/test_ip_coap.py tests/interface/test_messages.py tests/interface/test_framing.py tests/interface/test_serial.py tests/interface/test_tcp.py tests/interface/test_handler.py -q => 112 passed in 0.87s. uv run ruff check src/lichen/interface/__init__.py src/lichen/interface/messages.py src/lichen/interface/framing.py src/lichen/interface/serial.py src/lichen/interface/tcp.py src/lichen/interface/handler.py tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py => All checks passed. repo root: git diff --check => passed. No EC2 used; change is docs/Python contract tests and legacy comments only, no Zephyr build behavior changed.
Review evidence (2026-07-02): explorer Singer found spec/lichen-native conflicted with LCI CoAP and recommended classifying it historical/non-authoritative plus CoAP raw diagnostics. Explorer Volta mapped stale Python/C interface references and tests. Initial review Dewey found P2 auth table contradiction and P2 /diag/raw/rx status/event shape ambiguity; fixed by admin-only /diag/raw/* access and /diag/raw/rx/events Observe path, plus legacy docstring cleanup. Initial review Aquinas found P1 raw diagnostics auth contradiction; fixed by access table. Final review Pasteur found P2 non-normative raw diagnostics auth; fixed to 'MUST require local administrative authorization'. Final review Sartre found no P0-P2 and a below-P2 USB native wording nit; fixed to 'USB CDC-ACM serial/SLIP LCI'. Narrow re-reviews Pasteur and Sartre both reported no remaining P0-P2 findings.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware reconciliation: spec/11-lci.md is authoritative IPv6+CoAP LCI; spec/lichen-native and legacy Python/C CBOR native surfaces are marked historical/non-authoritative; optional admin-only /diag/raw/* CoAP model documented; Python/docs contract guard added; follow-ups created for raw diagnostics UI/client implementation and legacy CBOR lifecycle. Validation: 112 targeted Python tests passed, Ruff passed, git diff --check passed. Final re-reviews reported no P0-P2 findings.
